Title: Slotted ALOHA-based URA Scheme with Index Modulation for Short Block Length

Abstract: Unsourced random access (URA) has become a promising technique to support massive connectivity scenarios. Title: Construction of partially-doped generalized LDPC codes over regular LDPC codes
Authors: Jaewha Kim, Jae-Won Kim, Jong-Seon No
Abstract: In this paper, we propose a new code design technique for GLDPC codes which we call partial doping. Title: A MATE-GDBF Algorithm for Irregular Punctured LDPC Codes and Its Decoder Implementation
Authors: Xiao-Juan Huang, Li-Wei Liu, Yen-Chin Liao, Hsie-Chia Chang, Sau-Gee Chen
Abstract: This paper proposes a MATE-GDBF decoding algorithm and a corresponding decoder architecture for punctured LDPC codes. Title: Performance of PSA-EKF Phase Noise Compensation in 3GPP Phase Noise Models for Mobile Backhaul Links
Authors: Ryota Kuribayashi, Mamoru Sawahashi, Norifumi Kamiya
Abstract: The time-varying phase noise (PN) due to a local oscillator of a base station and a set of user equipment (UE) is a major impairment to the backhaul and access links employing millimeter-wave bands. Authors: Takeshi Matsumura, Hikaru Kawasaki, Kazuo Ibuka
Abstract: The Society 5.0 will be strongly assisted by cyber-physical systems (CSP) with collecting various data in a physical space, analyzing them in a cyber space, and actuating physical devices. Title: Binary QC-LDPC Codes Based on Whiteman's Generalized Cyclotomy
Authors: Tao Wang, Zhiping Shi, Li Deng, Juan Yang
Abstract: This study introduces a construction method for QC-LDPC codes, ensuring their absence of four-cycles by utilizing Whiteman's generalized cyclotomic (GC) classes. An analysis of the cycle structure within the Tanner graph for the proposed codes, specifically focusing on cycles with length six, is conducted. Title: Viterbi Soft Demodulation Algorithm for CPFSK Systems
Authors: Changheng Wang, Xinyu Wang, Kun Jiang, Jun Zou
Abstract: Continuous Phase Frequency Shift Keying (CPFSK) is a phase-continuous constant-envelope modulation scheme. In modern communication systems, Turbo codes or LDPC codes are commonly used as error control codes to combat the effects of channel noise and fading. Title: LLR Metrics for 16K-QAM Soft-Decision: Implementation in IEEE 802.11bn (Wi-Fi 8)
Authors: Roger Pierre Fabris Hoefel
Abstract: The IEEE 802.11 Ultra High Reliability (UHR) Study Group (SG) was created in July 2022 to develop the 802.11bn amendment (Wi-Fi 8). Title: Adaptive Modulation and Coding for Underwater Acoustic Communication Based on Neural Networks
Authors: Jixing Zheng, Yu Yuan, Yan Wei, Xingbin Tu, Xuesong Lu, Shaojian Yang, Fengzhong Qu
Abstract: Due to the complexity and variability of the marine environment, the modulation and coding schemes used in underwater acoustic communication do not match the actual channel, which can lead to a decrease in communication accuracy or an increase in power consumption. Title: A Low Complexity Turbo MMSE Equalization Method for OTFS Underwater Acoustic Communications
Authors: Qingsong Wang, Nan Zhao, Lianyou Jing, Chengbing He, Chaopeng Zhou
Abstract: This paper provides a low complexity turbo iterative equalization algorithm for OTFS underwater acoustic communication systems. Abstract: In this paper, we analyze the performances of the fifth telecommunication generation (5G) new radio (NR) quasi-cyclic (QC) low-density parity check (QC-LDPC) codes via density evolution (DE) for base graph 2 (BG2) over binary erasure channel (BEC). Title: CVQKD System with Variable SNR Based on Variable Code Rate LDPC
Authors: Jiaxu Wen, Xuchao Liu
Abstract: In this paper, a comprehensive overview of Gaussian data modulation and error correction negotiation in Continuous Variable Quantum Key Distribution (CVQKD) is presented. Title: Vehicle Density in mmWave 5G V2X Highway Communication Systems: A Channel Coding Approach
Authors: Dimitrios Chatzoulis, Costas Chaikalis, Dimitrios Kosmanos, Apostolos Xenakis, Kostas Anagnostou
